figures.caption: Table 5: Per-repository branch coverage on TestGenEval Lite by model. Each cell averages across modules in that repo. Mean row shows the per-module average. Best per group in bold.
Figure 7: Mean branch coverage by repository on RepoExploreBench, averaged for all models. CovQValue outperforms all baselines on every repository.
Table 2: Mean per-module branch coverage (± standard error) for all 3 models. CovQValue vs. Greedy comparisons: p < 0.0001 (paired t-test). d: Cohen’s d effect size.

Figure 2: Cumulative branch coverage over execution steps, averaged across three models (Gemini 3 Flash, GPT-5.4 Mini, Mistral Large). CovQValue (ours) continues discovering branches while Random and Greedy stall earlier. Shaded region indicates ±1 SE.

Figure 6: Corridor navigation on four RepoExploreBench modules (Gemini 3 Flash). It showcases how Random or Greedy remain flat while CovQValue traverses setup corridors to reach deep branches as the number of steps increase.
Table 4: Line coverage and pass rate. Results consistent with branch coverage.
Table 3: RepoExploreBench composition. Packages selected from top 150 PyPI downloads (as of March 2026). Corridor structure describes the sequential dependencies that gate access to deep branches.

Figure 4: Trade-off between test pass rate and branch coverage. Large dots represent the mean across models, while individual models and benchmark results are represent with transparent dots. Coverage-map strategies discover more branches by generating larger tests at a small cost in pass rate.

Figure 3: Mean branch coverage by repository on TestGenEval Lite, averaged across three models. CovQValue outperforms other methods on every repository, with the largest gains over Greedy on sympy (+55%), matplotlib (+52%), and astropy (+55%).
Table 1: Formal mapping from Sun et al. (2011) optimal Bayesian exploration framework to LLM-based test generation.

Figure 5: Ablation studies on RepoExploreBench (93 targets, Gemini 3 Flash). (a) CovQValue scales steadily with budget. (b) Longer plans yield higher coverage, confirming the benefit of multi-step lookahead. (c) Top: discount factor sensitivity (default in bold). Bottom: component contributions.

Figure 1: Overview of CovQvalue. At each round, the LLM generates K diverse candidate plans conditioned on the coverage map. Each plan is scored by its estimated Q-value, information gain plus discounted future reachability (Equation 2), and the highest scoring plan is executed. The resulting branch coverage updates the coverage map for next round.
Table 6: Per-repository branch coverage on RepoExploreBench by model. Each cell averages across modules in that repo. Mean row shows the per-module average Best per group in bold.
Figure 11: Cumulative branch coverage over execution steps on RepoExploreBench plotted individually for each model.
Figure 9: Cumulative branch coverage over execution steps on TestGenEval Lite plotted individually for each model.
